subject: Human Resources
title: The Business Case for Engagement
description: One-minute skill: The Business Case for Engagement.

hook.text: Companies with highly engaged teams see 21% higher profitability. Engagement isn't feel-good—it's financial.

buildup.text: Engaged teams have lower turnover, fewer safety incidents, higher customer ratings, and greater productivity.

discovery.text: Gallup estimates disengagement costs the global economy $8.8 trillion per year in lost productivity.

twist.text: Yet most companies treat engagement as an HR project—not a business strategy. It stays siloed and underfunded.

climax.text: When leaders see engagement data alongside revenue data, they invest differently—and results follow.

punchline.text: Engagement isn't soft. It's $8.8 trillion hard.

quiz.question: How much does global disengagement cost the economy annually?
